channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: I built a free GPA & student loan calculator site with pure HTML/CSS/JS — here's what I learned
item.description: I recently launched GPA Vault — a free tool site for US college students with 15 calculators and 16 blog guides covering GPA, student loans, and college costs. No framework, no backend, no...

item.title: Novo CNPJ: O "Bug do Milênio" versão Tupiniquim?
item.description: Se você é dev no Brasil, o número 14 está no seu DNA. Mas o alicerce mudou. O estoque de combinações numéricas do CNPJ esgotou (abrimos 4,2 milhões de empresas só em 2024!) e a Receita Federal deu...

item.title: I built a free, browser-only European payment QR generator (and an MCP server)
item.description: Every time I get an invoice or a broker deposit instruction, I do the same dumb, error-prone thing: copy the IBAN, the amount, and the variable symbol into my banking app by hand. One transposed...

item.title: Track Page Views using Application Insights
item.description: To track page views in an Angular application using Azure Application Insights, you typically integrate the Application Insights JavaScript SDK and hook it into Angular routing events. Below is a...

item.title: Securing Third-Party SaaS with Federation and SCIM
item.description: Choosing the federation pattern that minimizes risk and friction Designing SCIM-based automated provisioning that scales Mapping roles and enforcing least privilege in third-party SaaS Eliminating...

item.title: I benchmarked my own semantic cache against RedisVL and Upstash for a week. Here is what actually held up
item.description: Most semantic cache benchmarks are a vendor showing you the one dataset where they win, on a model they finetuned, against a competitor they configured badly. You read it, you nod, you learn...

item.title: Rethinking Lakehouse Architecture Through Data Ownership: AWS vs. Snowflake
item.description: Original Japanese article: データの主導権から考えるAWSとSnowflakeのレイクハウスアーキテクチャ Introduction I'm Aki, an AWS Community Builder (@jitepengin). When designing a data platform, discussions about whether to lean...

item.title: Meme Monday
item.description: Meme Monday! Today's cover image comes from the last thread. DEV is an inclusive space! Humor in poor taste will be downvoted by mods.

item.title: I built a container escape audit tool — here's what v4.0 adds
item.description: canonical_url: https://github.com/liamromanis101/K8s-container_escape_audit Container security tooling tends to fall into two camps: heavyweight scanners that run outside the container before...

item.title: Fortifying Your Linux Bastion: A Comprehensive Guide to Intrusion Detection with Fail2Ban
item.description: In the vast, interconnected expanse of the internet, every publicly accessible Linux server is a potential target. Within minutes of being connected to the network, your server will be probed and...

item.title: Microsoft could be the next Big Tech antitrust target
item.description: Over the past several years, Microsoft has largely managed to withstand populist calls to break up Big Tech while peers faced sweeping lawsuits. But a probe by the Federal Trade Commission...

item.title: Beyond LLMs: Why Scalable Enterprise AI Adoption Depends on Agent Logic

item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. China has approved the world’s first invasive...

item.title: The Security Growth Platform: Why MSPs Are Moving Beyond vCISO Tools
item.description: Three years ago, the practical question for an MSP building a cybersecurity practice was which "vCISO platform" to buy. The term was good shorthand for the work at the time: assessments, advisory,...

item.title: These are the first Nvidia RTX Spark laptops
item.description: Nvidia has officially entered the world of consumer laptop chips with the RTX Spark, and several device makers already have hardware lined up for it. Microsoft, Asus, HP, MSI, Lenovo, and Dell are...

item.title: Norse Atlantic Airways Offers Dirt-Cheap Tickets. There’s a Catch
item.description: Dozens of people have complained to the Federal Trade Commission about Norse Atlantic Airways’ tech-first customer service operation. Some said they lost thousands of dollars.

item.title: Asus just announced the OLED Xbox Ally X of my dreams
item.description: If you asked me what I'd change about the Xbox Ally X handheld - aside from fixing Windows, I mean - I'd tell you two key things. First, give me a bigger, better screen. Even a little bit bigger,...

item.title: OpenAI Codex Authentication Tokens Stolen in codexui-android npm Supply Chain Attack
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new malicious supply chain campaign that's targeting developers using OpenAI Codex through a legitimate-looking remote web UI. The tool, named...

item.title: China has approved the world’s first invasive brain-computer chip—here’s what’s next
item.description: One day last October, sitting in the courtyard of his house in China’s Henan province, Dong Hui decided to see if he could hold a pen to write. Dong, 39, had sustained spinal cord injuries in a...

item.title: Critical WP Maps Pro Flaw Actively Exploited to Create Admin Accounts
item.description: Threat actors are attempting to actively exploit a critical security flaw impacting WP Maps Pro, a WordPress plugin that has had over 15,000 sales on the Envato Market, to create malicious...

item.title: Welcome NVIDIA Cosmos 3: The First Open Omni-model for Physical AI Reasoning and Action
This is the Microsoft Surface Laptop Ultra with Nvidia RTX Spark
item.description: Once upon a time, Microsoft had to write off $900 million betting an Arm-based Nvidia chip could power its first flagship Windows portable, the original Microsoft Surface. But today, it's trying...

item.title: Nvidia announces RTX Spark as ‘the most efficient PC chip ever built’
item.description: This fall, Nvidia will officially become a consumer PC chipmaker like Intel, AMD, Apple, and Qualcomm, putting a complete computing chip - not just graphics - into the very heart of laptops and...

item.title: AMD’s new pitch: our old tech is so good you should just keep using it
item.description: Computex 2026 is underway in Taiwan, and we're expecting all manner of flashy computers with jaw-dropping prices (or no prices at all) as the entire industry navigates RAMageddon. But for desktop...

item.title: The QD-OLED gaming monitor that started it all got a big upgrade
item.description: Alienware is taking to this year's Computex 2026 in Taipei to announce some cool gaming monitors, most notably two exciting OLED options that are coming at different points this year. First off,...

item.title: Dell is bringing back the XPS 13 as a MacBook Neo competitor — with a temporary discount to $599
item.description: Dell is making good on its tease from CES and finally announcing a new XPS 13. The XPS 13 returns as a budget-friendly option, launching in July at a promotional student price of $599 - though...

item.title: Apple’s strategy for smart glasses is the same as for smart watches
item.description: Apple isn't just looking to take on Meta in the smart glasses market; it's looking to upend eyewear as a whole, according to Bloomberg's Mark Gurman. When the Apple Watch launched, it wasn't...

item.title: Erin Brockovich takes aim at data center secrecy
item.description: Environmental activist Erin Brockovich has a new mission.

item.title: How to watch Nvidia’s Computex keynote
item.description: NVIDIA's CEO Jensen Huang is set to take the stage for his GTC Taipei keynote at 8PM PT / 11PM ET. You can watch all the announcements here and embedded below. Rumors have been flying about what...

item.title: Making sense of the debate over AI psychosis
item.description: On the latest episode of Equity, we debate whether tech CEOs are "uniquely prone to AI psychosis."
